parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ messaging_product: "whatsapp", to: $json.senderPhone, type: "text", text: { body: $json.ackText } }) }}

parameters.jsCode: return [{ json: { finalText: String($input.first().json.text || ''), isVoice: true } }];

parameters.description:
Search clients in the CGPE database by name (fuzzy, case-insensitive), phoneLast10, dob, or dob month-day for birthday lists. Legacy LIC records also carry policy fields (policyNo, sumAssured, premium, maturityDate) directly on the client row. FORMATTING: When multiple results are found, ALWAYS number them: 1️⃣ Name | Detail
2️⃣ Name | Detail etc. Show total count at end: 'Total: X found'. For birthday/anniversary lists format as: 1️⃣ Name | Date | Phone
2️⃣ Name | Date | Phone. Returns {ok, count, results}.

Fetch insurance policies by clientName (fuzzy), phoneLast10, or policyNo. Returns ALL matching policies with type, insurer, sumAssured, premium, dueDate, maturityDate, status. FORMATTING: ALWAYS number each policy: 1️⃣ PolicyNo | Type | SA ₹X | Premium ₹X | Status
2️⃣ PolicyNo | Type | SA ₹X | Premium ₹X | Status. Show total at end. For maturity list: 1️⃣ Name | Policy | Est. Amount | Maturity Date. For premium due list: 1️⃣ Name | Policy | Premium Amt | Due Date. Highlight overdue premiums with ⚠️.

MANDATORY FIRST STEP before ANY create_ticket or create_claim call — call this EVERY TIME, no exceptions. Search by clientName OR phoneLast10 WITHOUT passing category, so you find ALL open tickets for this client regardless of topic. If ANY open ticket is found (count > 0): 1) Tell sender the existing ticket ID and its current details, 2) Ask if they want to update that ticket, 3) Use update_ticket to modify it. ONLY if this returns count:0 may you proceed to create_ticket. NEVER skip this step.

CRITICAL: You MUST call ticket_guard FIRST for this client before calling create_ticket. If ticket_guard returned ANY open ticket for the same client (count > 0), STOP — do NOT create a new ticket, use update_ticket on the existing one instead. Only create a new ticket if ticket_guard returned count:0. Categories: claim_health, claim_general, claim_life, claim_death, premium, renewal, maturity, portfolio_report, investment_sip, service_change, scan_copy, callback, meeting, task_assignment, complaint, lead_followup, other. kind: document_collection | claim_request | callback | info | general | task | meeting. Include originalCommand (exact transcript), aiUnderstanding, task, requiredItems, priority (P1-P4), zone (green/amber/red), source (admin/client), assignedTo.

value.params: ={{ /*n8n-auto-generated-fromAI-override*/ $fromAI('params_create_ticket', 'JSON string with category, kind, priority, zone, clientName, phoneLast10, policyNo, originalCommand, aiUnderstanding, task, requiredItems array, slots object, source') }}

parameters.description: Update an existing ticket by ticketId. PREFERRED over create_ticket when the client already has an open ticket — always use this instead of creating a duplicate. Use to: change state (new/info_pending/in_progress/awaiting_third_party/escalated/resolved/closed), update task description, add receivedItems, modify slots, change priority/zone, reassign via assignedTo, update policyNo, or add a note. When ticket_guard finds an existing ticket, use this tool to apply any changes the sender requested.

Create a claim request AFTER sender confirmation AND after required info/documents are in. REQUIRED SLOTS BY CLAIM TYPE: Health claims REQUIRE: hospitalName, patientName, admitDate, illness, doctorName. General/Motor claims REQUIRE: policyType, cause, incidentDate, vehicleNo (for motor only). Life/Death claims REQUIRE: patientName, deathDate, cause. If ANY required slot is missing, ASK the user for it before calling this tool — do NOT skip slots. Claim starts at stage documents_required. claimType: health | general | life | death | motor | maturity. CONVERSATION FLOW: 1) Identify claim type 2) Ask for ALL required slots one by one 3) Confirm with user 4) Call ticket_guard first 5) Then create_claim.

value.params: ={{ /*n8n-auto-generated-fromAI-override*/ $fromAI('params_create_claim', 'JSON string with claimType, clientName, phoneLast10, policyNo, ticketId, slots object {hospitalName, patientName, admitDate, illness, doctorName}') }}

parameters.description: Move a claim through stages: documents_required -> under_review -> company_email_sent -> amount_claimed -> amount_verification -> (shortfall_requery when received amount is short by more than 10 percent) -> settled -> closed. Can also set amounts {claimed, approved, received, shortfall} and a note. Admin confirmation required first.

value.params: ={{ /*n8n-auto-generated-fromAI-override*/ $fromAI('params_advance_claim', 'JSON string, e.g. {"claimId":"CGPE-C-1001","stage":"under_review","note":"docs verified"} or with amounts object') }}

parameters.description:
List records with pagination (5 per page). type=leads for active leads. type=claims with status pending for pending claims; filter by phoneLast10 for a client's own claims. type=tickets lists open tickets. Pass page for next pages. FORMATTING: ALWAYS number each record starting from ((page-1)*5 + 1): 1️⃣ Record details
2️⃣ Record details. Show 'Page X' and total count at the bottom. If more pages exist, mention 'Aglu page mate bolo'.

Generate the CGPE family financial and insurance report. Gathers ALL policies of the client's ENTIRE FAMILY (grouped by LIC family group), computes life/health cover, maturity timeline and premiums, and returns a report URL plus summary numbers. Use for 'maro report moklo', portfolio/report requests. Takes 30-60 seconds. RESPONSE FORMAT: You MUST share the PDF URL DIRECTLY as the FIRST thing in your reply, like: '📄 Your report is ready: {pdfUrl}' followed by a brief summary of summary numbers. NEVER summarize without giving the link. NEVER say 'report is being generated' if you already have the result. If the tool returns pdfUrl or url, ALWAYS include pdfUrl (or url) verbatim in your response.

Manage team operations: tasks, meetings, and assignments. CRITICAL MEMBER GUARD: Before calling {"action":"assign"} or {"action":"meeting"} for a person, you MUST FIRST call {"action":"list"} to verify they are an active team member! If the person (e.g. Rahul Gandhi) is NOT in the active list, DO NOT assign or create the meeting/task. Tell the sender the person is not in the team and list available members. Actions: {"action":"tasks","today":true} for today's tasks; {"action":"meeting","title":...,"dueAt":"YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm","details":...,"assigneeName":...} to add meeting; {"action":"list"} to list team members; {"action":"assign","assigneeName":...,"title":...,"ticketId":...} to assign task or ticket.

value.params: ={{ /*n8n-auto-generated-fromAI-override*/ $fromAI('params_team_ops', 'JSON string, e.g. {"action":"tasks","today":true} or {"action":"meeting","title":"Client meeting","dueAt":"2026-07-04T15:00"} or {"action":"assign","assigneeName":"Raj","title":"Follow up"}', 'string') }}

parameters.description:
Query CRM lists: birthdays, anniversaries, clients, maturities, and premium dues. Types: {"type":"birthday","day":4,"month":7} for birthdays; {"type":"anniversary","day":4,"month":7} for anniversaries; {"type":"clients","countOnly":true} or {"withPhone":true} for client list/count; {"type":"maturity","month":7,"year":2026} for maturity list; {"type":"dues","month":7} for premium renewal dues. Always use numbers for day and month. FORMATTING: ALWAYS number each record: 1️⃣ Name | Date | Phone
2️⃣ Name | Date | Phone. Show 'Total: X records' at end. For maturity: 1️⃣ Name | Policy | Est. ₹X | Date. For dues: 1️⃣ Name | Policy | ₹Premium | Due Date.
Get summary counts of tickets, claims, or leads. Types: {"type":"tickets"} for open ticket counts; {"type":"claims"} for pending claim counts; {"type":"leads"} for active lead counts.

Create a new sales/investment lead after confirming details with sender. Params: {"name":"Client Name","phone":"9876543210","interest":"SIP / Health Plan / Term Insurance","referredBy":"Referrer Name or Self"}. Always ask confirmation before calling.
Fetch stored policy documents and scan copies by client name, phoneLast10, or policyNo. Returns list of available document URLs and types.
